questions.stem: I ______ my homework already. I ______ it an hour ago.
questions.explanation: 第一空有already（已经），是现在完成时的标志词，强调对现在的影响（作业已完成），用have finished；第二空有具体过去时间状语an hour ago（一小时前），只能用一般过去时finished。口诀：already/ever/never用现完，具体过去时间用过去。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The boy ______ is standing under the tree is my brother.
questions.explanation: 先行词是the boy（人），且在定语从句中作主语，应用关系代词who。which指物；whose表示所属关系（谁的）；whom在从句中作宾语。本题从句缺主语，故选who。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: These books ______ to the library yesterday.
questions.explanation: 主语these books与return是被动关系（书被归还），应用被动语态be+过去分词。时间状语yesterday表明用一般过去时，主语是复数，故用were returned。A和D是主动语态；C时态错误。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem:
—Have you ever been to Beijing?
—Yes, I ______ there last summer.
questions.explanation: 答句有具体过去时间状语last summer（去年夏天），只能用一般过去时went。现在完成时不能与具体过去时间状语（yesterday, last week, in 2020等）连用。have gone表示去了未回，不符合语境。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: We should ______ our best to protect the environment.
questions.explanation: 固定搭配try one's best to do sth.（尽某人最大努力做某事）。do one's best也可，但选项B是do，缺少one's；make表示制作；take表示拿取，均不与best搭配表示尽力。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: The old bridge is not strong enough to support heavy trucks. It may ______ if too many trucks cross it at the same time.
questions.explanation: collapse指（建筑物）倒塌、坍塌，符合桥梁因承重过大而倒塌的语境。fall指跌落（从高处落下）；drop指掉落（不经意）；break down指机器故障或身体垮掉，不用于建筑物倒塌。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: If you are ______ with your current score, you should work harder to get a better one next time.
questions.explanation: be satisfied with是固定搭配，意为对……满意。根据后半句work harder（更努力），可推断前半句是指如果对现状满意。exciting（令人兴奋的）和interesting（有趣的）修饰物；surprised（惊讶的）与语境不符。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.explanation: take part in指参加活动并在其中发挥作用，后接project（项目）。join指加入组织或团体成为成员（如join the club）；join in多指参加小规模活动（如join in the game）；attend指出席会议、上课等正式场合。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a new project called "Green Campus". As a member of the environmental club, I was excited to (8)______ it. Our goal was to reduce waste and save energy in our daily school life.

At first, many students didn't understand why we needed to change their habits. They kept throwing rubbish everywhere and forgot to turn off lights when leaving classrooms. (9)______, we didn't give up. We designed some interesting posters and put them up around the school. We also organized a competition to see which class could save the most electricity in a month.

To our surprise, these activities worked really well. More and more students began to (10)______ the importance of protecting our environment. By the end of the semester, the results were amazing. The amount of waste in our school had dropped by 40%, and the electricity bill was much lower than before.

Our principal praised us at the school meeting. We felt very (11)______ and proud of what we had achieved. This experience taught me that small actions can make a big difference. If everyone does their part, we can create a better world for future generations. I decided to (12)______ the good work and encourage more classmates to join us.

questions.explanation: 前文提到学生不改变习惯、乱扔垃圾（负面情况），后文说我们没放弃（转折），应用However（然而）。Therefore（因此）表因果；Besides（此外）表递进；Instead（反而）表替代，均不符合上下文逻辑。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a new project called "Green Campus". As a member of the environmental club, I was excited to (8)______ it. Our goal was to reduce waste and save energy in our daily school life.

At first, many students didn't understand why we needed to change their habits. They kept throwing rubbish everywhere and forgot to turn off lights when leaving classrooms. (9)______, we didn't give up. We designed some interesting posters and put them up around the school. We also organized a competition to see which class could save the most electricity in a month.

To our surprise, these activities worked really well. More and more students began to (10)______ the importance of protecting our environment. By the end of the semester, the results were amazing. The amount of waste in our school had dropped by 40%, and the electricity bill was much lower than before.

Our principal praised us at the school meeting. We felt very (11)______ and proud of what we had achieved. This experience taught me that small actions can make a big difference. If everyone does their part, we can create a better world for future generations. I decided to (12)______ the good work and encourage more classmates to join us.

questions.explanation: realize意为意识到，符合语境学生开始意识到环保的重要性。believe（相信）虽可搭配，但realize更强调从不懂到懂的认识过程；doubt（怀疑）和regret（后悔）与语境相反。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a new project called "Green Campus". As a member of the environmental club, I was excited to (8)______ it. Our goal was to reduce waste and save energy in our daily school life.

At first, many students didn't understand why we needed to change their habits. They kept throwing rubbish everywhere and forgot to turn off lights when leaving classrooms. (9)______, we didn't give up. We designed some interesting posters and put them up around the school. We also organized a competition to see which class could save the most electricity in a month.

To our surprise, these activities worked really well. More and more students began to (10)______ the importance of protecting our environment. By the end of the semester, the results were amazing. The amount of waste in our school had dropped by 40%, and the electricity bill was much lower than before.

Our principal praised us at the school meeting. We felt very (11)______ and proud of what we had achieved. This experience taught me that small actions can make a big difference. If everyone does their part, we can create a better world for future generations. I decided to (12)______ the good work and encourage more classmates to join us.

questions.explanation: 前文提到To our surprise（令我们惊讶的是），此处felt very surprised与上文呼应，表示感到惊讶和自豪。disappointed（失望的）、frightened（害怕的）、bored（无聊的）均与取得成果的积极语境不符。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a new project called "Green Campus". As a member of the environmental club, I was excited to (8)______ it. Our goal was to reduce waste and save energy in our daily school life.

At first, many students didn't understand why we needed to change their habits. They kept throwing rubbish everywhere and forgot to turn off lights when leaving classrooms. (9)______, we didn't give up. We designed some interesting posters and put them up around the school. We also organized a competition to see which class could save the most electricity in a month.

To our surprise, these activities worked really well. More and more students began to (10)______ the importance of protecting our environment. By the end of the semester, the results were amazing. The amount of waste in our school had dropped by 40%, and the electricity bill was much lower than before.

Our principal praised us at the school meeting. We felt very (11)______ and proud of what we had achieved. This experience taught me that small actions can make a big difference. If everyone does their part, we can create a better world for future generations. I decided to (12)______ the good work and encourage more classmates to join us.

questions.explanation: keep up意为保持、继续（keep up the good work是固定表达，意为再接再厉）。give up（放弃）与语境相反；take up（开始从事/占据）；set up（建立），均不符合继续做好工作的语境。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:

questions.stem: What is the "20-20-20" rule according to the passage?
options: A. Exercise for 20 minutes every day to stay healthy.
B. Every 20 minutes, take a 20-second break and look at something 20 feet away.
C. Sleep 20 hours a week to rest your eyes.
D. Eat 20 types of vegetables to get enough vitamins.
questions.explanation: 细节理解题。根据第二段'every 20 minutes of looking at a screen, you should take a 20-second break and look at something at least 20 feet (about 6 meters) away'可知，B选项准确描述了该规则。A、C、D选项在文中均未提及。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解
questions.group_passage:
In today's digital age, many teenagers spend hours staring at (盯着看) screens every day. This has led to a rapid increase in eye problems among young people. Dr. Li Ming, a famous eye doctor from Beijing, has introduced a simple but effective rule to protect our eyes—the "20-20-20" rule.

According to Dr. Li, every 20 minutes of looking at a screen, you should take a 20-second break and look at something at least 20 feet (about 6 meters) away. This short break allows your eye muscles to relax and reduces the risk of eye strain (疲劳).

"Many students think they can just keep working for hours without rest, but that's very harmful," says Dr. Li. "The 20-20-20 rule is easy to remember and doesn't take much time, but it can make a huge difference in the long run."

A recent study showed that students who followed this rule regularly had 30% fewer complaints about dry eyes and headaches compared to those who didn't. Some schools in Shenzhen have even started reminding students to follow this rule during computer classes.

However, Dr. Li reminds us that the 20-20-20 rule is just one part of eye care. We should also make sure to spend time outdoors every day and maintain a healthy diet rich in vitamins A and C. If you experience persistent (持续的) eye discomfort, it's important to visit an eye doctor immediately.

questions.stem: What can we infer from Dr. Li Ming's words in Paragraph 3?
options: A. Students should study harder to avoid eye problems.
B. Small regular breaks are beneficial for eye health.
C. Eye problems cannot be prevented by any rules.
D. Working for hours without rest improves study efficiency.
questions.explanation: 推理判断题。根据第三段Dr. Li的话可知，连续工作数小时不休息是有害的，而20-20-20规则虽然每次只花20秒，但长期坚持有很大作用。由此可推断，规律的小休息对眼睛有益。A选项未提及；C选项与文意相反；D选项与文意相反（harmful）。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断
questions.group_passage:
In today's digital age, many teenagers spend hours staring at (盯着看) screens every day. This has led to a rapid increase in eye problems among young people. Dr. Li Ming, a famous eye doctor from Beijing, has introduced a simple but effective rule to protect our eyes—the "20-20-20" rule.

According to Dr. Li, every 20 minutes of looking at a screen, you should take a 20-second break and look at something at least 20 feet (about 6 meters) away. This short break allows your eye muscles to relax and reduces the risk of eye strain (疲劳).

"Many students think they can just keep working for hours without rest, but that's very harmful," says Dr. Li. "The 20-20-20 rule is easy to remember and doesn't take much time, but it can make a huge difference in the long run."

A recent study showed that students who followed this rule regularly had 30% fewer complaints about dry eyes and headaches compared to those who didn't. Some schools in Shenzhen have even started reminding students to follow this rule during computer classes.

However, Dr. Li reminds us that the 20-20-20 rule is just one part of eye care. We should also make sure to spend time outdoors every day and maintain a healthy diet rich in vitamins A and C. If you experience persistent (持续的) eye discomfort, it's important to visit an eye doctor immediately.

questions.stem: What does the word "strain" in Paragraph 2 probably mean?
questions.explanation: 词义猜测题。根据上下文，长时间看屏幕后眼睛肌肉需要放松（relax），可推断strain指眼睛疲劳。且后文第四段提到dry eyes and headaches（干眼和头痛），都是眼疲劳的症状。因此strain意为疲劳。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测
questions.group_passage:
In today's digital age, many teenagers spend hours staring at (盯着看) screens every day. This has led to a rapid increase in eye problems among young people. Dr. Li Ming, a famous eye doctor from Beijing, has introduced a simple but effective rule to protect our eyes—the "20-20-20" rule.

According to Dr. Li, every 20 minutes of looking at a screen, you should take a 20-second break and look at something at least 20 feet (about 6 meters) away. This short break allows your eye muscles to relax and reduces the risk of eye strain (疲劳).

"Many students think they can just keep working for hours without rest, but that's very harmful," says Dr. Li. "The 20-20-20 rule is easy to remember and doesn't take much time, but it can make a huge difference in the long run."

A recent study showed that students who followed this rule regularly had 30% fewer complaints about dry eyes and headaches compared to those who didn't. Some schools in Shenzhen have even started reminding students to follow this rule during computer classes.

However, Dr. Li reminds us that the 20-20-20 rule is just one part of eye care. We should also make sure to spend time outdoors every day and maintain a healthy diet rich in vitamins A and C. If you experience persistent (持续的) eye discomfort, it's important to visit an eye doctor immediately.

questions.stem: What is the best title for this passage?
options: A. The History of Eye Glasses
C. A Simple Rule for Eye Protection
D. The Importance of Outdoor Activities
questions.explanation: 主旨大意题。文章围绕20-20-20护眼法则展开，介绍了其内容、原理、效果及注意事项。C选项准确概括了文章主旨。A选项未提及；B选项范围过大；D选项只是文章末尾提到的细节，非主旨。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意
questions.group_passage:
In today's digital age, many teenagers spend hours staring at (盯着看) screens every day. This has led to a rapid increase in eye problems among young people. Dr. Li Ming, a famous eye doctor from Beijing, has introduced a simple but effective rule to protect our eyes—the "20-20-20" rule.

According to Dr. Li, every 20 minutes of looking at a screen, you should take a 20-second break and look at something at least 20 feet (about 6 meters) away. This short break allows your eye muscles to relax and reduces the risk of eye strain (疲劳).

"Many students think they can just keep working for hours without rest, but that's very harmful," says Dr. Li. "The 20-20-20 rule is easy to remember and doesn't take much time, but it can make a huge difference in the long run."

A recent study showed that students who followed this rule regularly had 30% fewer complaints about dry eyes and headaches compared to those who didn't. Some schools in Shenzhen have even started reminding students to follow this rule during computer classes.

However, Dr. Li reminds us that the 20-20-20 rule is just one part of eye care. We should also make sure to spend time outdoors every day and maintain a healthy diet rich in vitamins A and C. If you experience persistent (持续的) eye discomfort, it's important to visit an eye doctor immediately.

options: A. However, many of them don't know how to use their time wisely.
B. As a result, you'll be able to concentrate better on your studies.
C. Start with the most difficult or important tasks.
D. After a short rest, you'll return to work with fresh energy.
E. On the other hand, some students have plenty of time but no plan.
questions.explanation: 前文提到学生感到压力大、作业多时间少，后文给出建议，此处需填入承上启下的过渡句。A选项（然而，他们中许多人不知道如何明智地利用时间）承接上文的压力问题，引出下文的解决建议，逻辑通顺。E选项虽有转折，但plenty of time与前文too little time矛盾。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students feel stressed because they have too much homework and too little time. (17)______ Here are some useful tips to help you manage your time better.

First, make a to-do list every morning. Write down all the tasks you need to complete and put them in order of importance. (18)______ This way, you won't waste time deciding what to do next.

Second, avoid distractions while studying. Turn off your phone or put it in another room. (19)______ If you check your phone every few minutes, you'll lose your focus and take longer to finish your work.

Finally, don't forget to take short breaks. Your brain needs time to rest and process information. (20)______ Try the Pomodoro Technique: study for 25 minutes, then rest for 5 minutes.

By following these suggestions, you'll find that you can finish your homework more efficiently and still have time for hobbies and relaxation.

options: A. However, many of them don't know how to use their time wisely.
B. As a result, you'll be able to concentrate better on your studies.
C. Start with the most difficult or important tasks.
D. After a short rest, you'll return to work with fresh energy.
E. On the other hand, some students have plenty of time but no plan.
questions.explanation: 前文提到put them in order of importance（按重要性排序），C选项（从最困难或最重要的任务开始）承接上文，具体说明如何排序。后文This way指代先完成重要任务的做法，逻辑连贯。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students feel stressed because they have too much homework and too little time. (17)______ Here are some useful tips to help you manage your time better.

First, make a to-do list every morning. Write down all the tasks you need to complete and put them in order of importance. (18)______ This way, you won't waste time deciding what to do next.

Second, avoid distractions while studying. Turn off your phone or put it in another room. (19)______ If you check your phone every few minutes, you'll lose your focus and take longer to finish your work.

Finally, don't forget to take short breaks. Your brain needs time to rest and process information. (20)______ Try the Pomodoro Technique: study for 25 minutes, then rest for 5 minutes.

By following these suggestions, you'll find that you can finish your homework more efficiently and still have time for hobbies and relaxation.

options: A. However, many of them don't know how to use their time wisely.
B. As a result, you'll be able to concentrate better on your studies.
C. Start with the most difficult or important tasks.
D. After a short rest, you'll return to work with fresh energy.
E. On the other hand, some students have plenty of time but no plan.
questions.explanation: 前文建议关掉手机或放到另一个房间，B选项（因此，你将能够更专注于学习）是前文做法带来的结果，As a result表示因果关系。后文If you check...是反面论证，进一步说明为什么要远离手机干扰。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students feel stressed because they have too much homework and too little time. (17)______ Here are some useful tips to help you manage your time better.

First, make a to-do list every morning. Write down all the tasks you need to complete and put them in order of importance. (18)______ This way, you won't waste time deciding what to do next.

Second, avoid distractions while studying. Turn off your phone or put it in another room. (19)______ If you check your phone every few minutes, you'll lose your focus and take longer to finish your work.

Finally, don't forget to take short breaks. Your brain needs time to rest and process information. (20)______ Try the Pomodoro Technique: study for 25 minutes, then rest for 5 minutes.

By following these suggestions, you'll find that you can finish your homework more efficiently and still have time for hobbies and relaxation.

options: A. However, many of them don't know how to use their time wisely.
B. As a result, you'll be able to concentrate better on your studies.
C. Start with the most difficult or important tasks.
D. After a short rest, you'll return to work with fresh energy.
E. On the other hand, some students have plenty of time but no plan.
questions.explanation: 本段主题是take short breaks（短暂休息），D选项（短暂休息后，你会以全新的精力回到工作中）解释了为什么要休息，与主题一致。后文提到的Pomodoro Technique（番茄工作法）也是关于休息的具体方法，进一步印证D选项正确。

questions.group_passage:
